name: Conditions Precedent Checklist
description: Builds a categorized conditions-precedent checklist from a credit or financing agreement and delivers it as a landscape Word document. Each CP category gets its own heading and a four-column tracking table (Index, Clause Number, Clause, Status) with Status left blank for the deal team to complete at closing.
promptTemplate:
Prepare a conditions-precedent (CP) checklist for the following financing matter.

Matter: {{description}}

Documents provided: {{document}}

Instructions:
1. Work through the credit/financing agreement and identify every condition precedent — the main CP clause, any CP schedule, conditions to further utilisations, and any conditions subsequent.
2. Group the conditions into categories. Follow the agreement's own schedule headings where it provides them; otherwise use natural groupings such as corporate authorisations, finance documents, security documents, legal opinions, financial and other information, and KYC/regulatory.
3. Record each condition faithfully — quote or closely paraphrase the operative requirement — together with its clause, schedule, or paragraph reference.
4. Deliver the checklist using the docx_generate tool as a LANDSCAPE Word document (landscape: true). Emit one section per category: the category name as the section heading, followed by a table with exactly four columns — Index, Clause Number, Clause, Status. Number Index sequentially from 1 within each category, put the clause/schedule reference in Clause Number, the condition text in Clause, and leave every Status cell blank so the deal team can track satisfaction during closing.
5. Report the output file path, and flag separately any conditions you could not confidently categorise or whose scope is ambiguous.
